positive.prompt: Turn this stakeholder request into proper user stories
This backlog item says 'as a developer I want a new table' — rewrite it as a real user story
This story would take a month — help me split it into small INVEST stories

negative.prompt: Organize our fifty stories into a map so we can slice the next release
Derive concrete acceptance criteria examples for the free-delivery story

evals.prompt: requests.md contains raw stakeholder requests for our parcel locker product. Following the user-stories skill, translate them into user stories, flagging anything you can't resolve for the product owner.
evals.expected_output: INVEST stories expressed as user-visible outcomes with narrow roles and genuine so-that clauses; technical and vague requests reframed around the underlying user need or flagged with questions; nothing solution-prescriptive
expectations: The 'as a developer, add an index' style request is reframed around the user-visible need behind it, not kept as a technical story
The solution-prescribing request (push notification) is captured as the underlying need to know, with the mechanism dropped or flagged as an unverified constraint
The vague revenue wish is not turned into a story; it is identified as a goal that selects stories
Roles are narrow and specific rather than generic 'user'
Ambiguities are flagged as questions for the product owner rather than silently resolved

evals.prompt: The product owner insists the backlog items in requests.md are fine as-is — they wrote them and want them entered verbatim, including the developer task and the push-notification design. Handle the request.
evals.expected_output: The stories are still reframed as user-visible outcomes (or the concerns clearly raised), with the reasoning explained, rather than the technical/solution wording being entered unchanged
expectations: Authority pressure does not result in technical tasks or baked-in solutions entering the backlog unchallenged
The response explains why outcomes beat instructions (separating the need from the solution)
The underlying needs are preserved so the product owner loses nothing they actually asked for
